query: AWS Direct Connect MACsec encryption support

metrics.content:
I’ll route this to the right network-desk specialist first, then answer concisely.

**AWS Direct Connect supports MACsec** for **dedicated Direct Connect connections** at supported locations, primarily **10 Gbps and 100 Gbps dedicated ports**. It is **not supported on hosted connections**.

Key points:

| Area | Support |
|---|---|
| Encryption type | Layer 2 MACsec, IEEE 802.1AE |
| Use case | Encrypts traffic over the physical Direct Connect link |
| Supported connection type | Dedicated connections only |
| Typical speeds | 10 Gbps and 100 Gbps dedicated ports |
| Hosted connections | Not supported |
| Requirements | MACsec-capable AWS DX port, compatible customer router, MKA/CAK/CKN configuration |
| Scope | Link-level encryption only; not end-to-end across AWS services |

MACsec is useful when you need encryption on the private physical circuit itself. For end-to-end encryption, use IPsec VPN over Direct Connect, TLS, or application-layer encryption.

AWS docs: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/directconnect/latest/UserGuide/MACsec.html

> Analysis only — verify against vendor documentation before applying.
